description: Develop eBPF/XDP hooks for early packet discard on the mesh interface. XDP_DROP for unrecognized EtherTypes, token-bucket MAC rate limiting for DoS defense, and dynamic Bloom filter evaluation against eBPF maps before allocating userspace resources. Depends on MT7996 XDP support maturing in OpenWRT kernel. Phase 3 optimization from Gemini research.
title: eBPF/XDP pre-userspace broadcast filtering

description: Three new CID config fields have no UCI bindings: inference_model_gguf_cid (GGUF model hash), inference_model_tokenizer_cid (tokenizer hash), engram_manifest_cid (Engram manifest hash). All are Option<String> hex-encoded 32-byte CIDs. On embedded routers inference is unlikely (needs candle + large model), but operators experimenting with x86_64 OpenWRT VMs or powerful ARM64 boards may want it. Add UCI options (commented out by default), TOML generation, hex validation in init script. Gate behind a comment block explaining the inference feature requirement.
title: Add UCI config for inference and Engram CIDs

description: If the Harmony daemon detects anomalous behavior from a mesh peer (excessive invalid signatures, broadcast flooding), issue an IPC command to hostapd to hardware-ban the offending MAC address. Uses mesh11sd or direct hostapd control socket. Defense-in-depth against DoS on public-PSK mesh.
title: Dynamic MAC blacklisting for DoS mitigation

description: Define custom multicast MAC address space (e.g., 01:00:5E:80:XX:XX) to logically separate Reticulum announces, Zenoh scouts, and CAS Bloom filters into distinct L2 channels. Allows hardware MAC filter tables to drop irrelevant channels before CPU interrupt. Requires raw Ethernet transport (openwrt AF_PACKET transport) to be implemented first.
title: Group-addressed MAC channelization for protocol traffic

description: PKG_SOURCE_VERSION in harmony-node/Makefile points to 2fc9f06f, which predates tunnel infrastructure, discovery, JCS, credential import, and dozens of other features. Update to latest main commit and rebuild.
title: Update PKG_SOURCE_VERSION to latest harmony main

description: The rawlink_interface config field (Option<String>) specifies a Linux network interface name for raw Ethernet transport (AF_PACKET). This is a prerequisite for several existing beads: harmony-openwrt-1ky (group-addressed MAC channelization) and harmony-openwrt-z3r (fixed frame padding) both require raw Ethernet transport. Add UCI option, TOML generation, and note in README. Requires the rawlink Cargo feature to be useful — consider whether the OpenWRT build should enable it. The feature needs CAP_NET_RAW, so the init script may need capability adjustments or the binary needs to run with appropriate permissions for this interface.
title: Add UCI config for rawlink_interface

description: The harmony-node binary is described as '~3 MB static' in README. With 81 new commits adding harmony-runtime, harmony-speculative, harmony-zenoh (unconditional deps), plus build.rs WASM compilation, the binary may have grown. Cross-compile for aarch64-unknown-linux-musl and x86_64-unknown-linux-musl with current HEAD, measure stripped binary size, compare against the 396f97e baseline. If significantly larger, investigate which new crates contribute most (cargo bloat or manual feature toggling). Update README if the size claim changed. Target: still under 5 MB for flash-constrained devices.
title: Binary size audit after 81-commit delta

description: Remove the WAN firewall restriction for Harmony traffic. The current firewall blocks UDP 4242 on WAN — but with Harmony's E2E encryption (Reticulum, ML-KEM, ML-DSA), there is no meaningful trust difference between LAN and WAN. Any Harmony/Zenoh/Reticulum peer on the other side of the WAN port should 'just work'. Implementation: allow Harmony protocol traffic (UDP 4242 for Reticulum, Zenoh scouting ports, iroh-net QUIC) through the WAN zone. The firewall disappears for Harmony connections while remaining active for non-Harmony traffic.
title: Firewall-free WAN for Harmony protocols

close_reason: Already implemented. tunnel_peer UCI list exists in conf (line 19), init script emits [[tunnels]] sections with node_id (line 131-137), and upstream harmony core has full tunnel support (tunnel_task.rs, tunnel_bridge.rs, harmony-contacts). Only gap: optional name field on TunnelEntry not exposed via UCI — minor follow-up if needed.
description: The --tunnel-peer CLI flag enables outbound tunnel connections but is incomplete upstream (needs contact store for ML-KEM encapsulation, tracked as harmony core Bead #3). Once the upstream feature is wired, add tunnel_peer option to UCI config and init script. Blocked on harmony core contact store implementation.
title: UCI option for --tunnel-peer outbound initiator

description: The Makefile pins PKG_SOURCE_VERSION to 396f97e which is 81 harmony-node commits behind current HEAD (73f526a). This delta includes: harmony-runtime crate extraction, inference streaming (token mode, 0x03 API), external KV cache, full Engram pipeline (6 PRs), PlatformAdapter trait, speculative TOPLOC, and dozens of bug fixes. Update the pin, verify cargo fetch + build still works for both aarch64 and x86_64 targets, and bump PKG_RELEASE.
title: Bump PKG_SOURCE_VERSION to harmony HEAD (81 commits behind)

description: harmony-node now supports TOML config files (harmony-r4o). The init script should generate a TOML config from UCI options instead of passing CLI flags, enabling richer configuration (nested structures, lists) that CLI flags can't express.
title: Generate TOML config from UCI

description: Introduce 1-4 second random delay before responding to Zenoh queries and Reticulum route requests on the mesh interface. Breaks timing correlation that allows passive observers to map cause-and-effect packet sequences across the mesh. Already partially implemented in harmony-node (stochastic dialing for tunnel peers) — extend to mesh broadcast responses.
title: Stochastic transmission jitter for traffic analysis resistance

description: The mesh VIF currently bridges to br-lan (network='lan'), giving any device with the well-known PSK full LAN access. Create a dedicated 'harmony' network interface with its own subnet, DHCP pool, and firewall zone that only permits Harmony protocol ports (UDP 4242, 7446, 4434-4435) and blocks access to other LAN services. Requires harmony-node to bind to the mesh interface specifically or route between zones.
title: Isolate HARMONY-MESH from br-lan with dedicated network/firewall zone

description: Add mesh_rssi_threshold to reject peering with weak-signal nodes. Complements mcast_rate=24000 which culls weak links at the frame level — RSSI threshold prevents peering establishment in the first place. Needs research on appropriate dBm value for 5GHz outdoor/indoor mesh.
title: RSSI threshold for mesh peering quality

close_reason: Created in wrong repo, recreated as harmony-os-4zg
description:
Sans-I/O framebuffer driver for the RPi5's VideoCore VII GPU. Minimal scope: linear framebuffer via mailbox property interface (no 3D, no hardware cursor, no vsync).

Key components:
- Mailbox property interface: request framebuffer allocation, set resolution/depth/pitch
- FramebufferDriver state machine: sans-I/O, returns FramebufferAction variants
- Pixel writing: direct memory-mapped writes to the linear framebuffer
- 9P FramebufferServer: expose as /dev/fb0 with ctl (resolution/depth) and data (raw pixel writes)

BCM2712 VideoCore VII uses the same mailbox property interface as BCM2835/2711. The framebuffer is allocated by the firmware and returned as a physical address. No GPU command submission needed for 2D.

Reference: https://github.com/raspberrypi/firmware/wiki/Mailbox-property-interface

Independent of the hypervisor chain. Estimated: ~200 lines of driver code.
title: Native Rust framebuffer driver for RPi5 VideoCore VII

description: Configure a unified BSS color across all Harmony mesh nodes to enable spatial reuse (802.11ax). Nodes treat alien transmissions from neighboring non-Harmony networks as background noise, reducing unnecessary CSMA/CA backoff. Requires Wi-Fi 6 hardware (MT7996). Add he_bss_color UCI option to auto-config.
title: BSS Coloring for dense mesh deployments

close_reason: All dependencies closed: auto-mesh (im5), firewall (4jl), broadcast (ua3). Plus power save (yw6) and network isolation (979) shipped.
description: The end goal: install the harmony-node package on any OpenWRT device, and it immediately joins the communal HARMONY-MESH WiFi, opens WAN for Harmony peers, and starts participating in the decentralized network. No manual WiFi configuration, no firewall rules to edit, no CLI flags to set. This is the integration bead that ties together auto-join mesh, firewall-free WAN, and optimized broadcast. The uci-defaults script should handle all setup on first install.
title: Zero-config Harmony mesh: install package → mesh works

description: The UCI config has no options for --relay-url, --tunnel-peer, or tunnel entries. These are needed for internet-connected mesh nodes that peer over iroh-net tunnels. Add relay_url, tunnel_peer list options to /etc/config/harmony-node and wire them into the init script.
title: Tunnel and relay UCI configuration

description: Add a LuCI app (luci-app-harmony-node) for web-based configuration. Show service status, identity info, peer count, cache stats. Allow editing all UCI options. Stretch: mesh peer visualization.
title: LuCI web interface for harmony-node

description: DEPENDS:=@(aarch64) restricts to aarch64 only. Many OpenWRT devices and VMs are x86_64. Add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl as a supported target, make architecture selectable in the Makefile.
title: x86_64 target support

description: Add mipsel_24kc and arm_cortex-a7 targets for common OpenWRT devices (TP-Link, Ubiquiti, etc.). Requires testing cross-compilation for these targets and possibly disabling features that don't build on 32-bit.
title: Multi-arch support (mipsel, arm)

description: Every Harmony-OpenWRT device should automatically create/join an 802.11s mesh interface with SSID 'HARMONY-MESH' and PSK 'ZEBLITHIC'. This is intentionally well-known — Harmony provides E2E encryption (Reticulum + ML-KEM), so the WiFi layer is just transport. The mesh should auto-configure on install: add the mesh VIF to the 5GHz radio, set mesh_fwding=0 (Reticulum handles routing), set mcast_rate=24000. This replaces the current manual setup guide with zero-touch mesh peering. Any Harmony device within radio range automatically joins the mesh.
title: Auto-join HARMONY-MESH communal WiFi network

description: WiFi A-MSDU/A-MPDU aggregation doesn't work for broadcast frames (no BlockAck mechanism). The Harmony stack must perform its own batching: buffer outgoing Zenoh publishes and NDN announcements, concatenate into single 1024-byte payload blocks before transmission. Reduces total frame count and PHY preamble overhead.
title: Application-layer frame batching for broadcast efficiency

description: Two core operational knobs missing from UCI/init script: (1) disk_quota (Option<String>, e.g. '10 GiB') — without this, CAS book storage is unbounded which is dangerous on small flash/USB. (2) logging.level (Option<String>) — currently only configurable via RUST_LOG env var, no UCI binding. Add UCI options, TOML generation in init script, and defaults in harmony-node.conf. disk_quota should default to commented-out (unbounded), logging level should default to 'info'.
title: Add UCI config for disk_quota and logging level

description: On the HARMONY-MESH network, broadcast traffic is useful — CAS/NDN means overhearing content announcements, Bloom filters, and Zenoh scouting benefits all participants. Configure the mesh interface for broadcast-friendly operation: elevated mcast_rate, optimized beacon interval, tuned multicast-to-unicast settings. Investigate whether Zenoh's multicast scouting can leverage WiFi broadcast directly (UDP multicast → WiFi broadcast) without IP layer overhead. Research: can we use raw 802.11 data frames for Zenoh/Reticulum instead of IP, making the broadcast domain a native content-addressed network?
title: Broadcast-optimized WiFi for CAS/NDN content sharing

description: TunnelEntry gained an optional 'name' field (Option<String>) for human-readable tunnel peer labels. The init script currently emits only node_id in [[tunnels]] sections. Extend UCI list format to support 'node_id:name' or add a parallel UCI list for tunnel names. Update TOML generation to emit 'name = "..."' when present. This is quality-of-life for operators managing multiple tunnel peers — names appear in logs and status output.
title: Support tunnel peer name in UCI config

description: 802.11 power save causes DTIM buffering delays for broadcast/multicast frames. Harmony mesh nodes are always-on routers — power save is actively harmful. Add 'iw dev mesh0 set power_save off' to the init script or uci-defaults. Simple but important for broadcast latency.
title: Disable power save on mesh interface

description: The init script (harmony-node.init) generates TOML from UCI options with shell string escaping, integer validation, conditional emission, and section ordering. Zero tests exist. A single bad escape or missing newline = bricked service on remote router. Create a test harness that: (1) sources the init script helper functions (_bool, _toml_str, _int) in a test shell, (2) verifies generated TOML parses correctly for various UCI input combinations, (3) tests edge cases: empty strings, strings with quotes/backslashes, zero/negative integers, missing UCI options, (4) verifies [[tunnels]] sections appear after root-level keys, (5) verifies conditional emission (mdns_stale_timeout only when mDNS enabled, relay_url only when non-empty). Could use a simple shell test script or a Python TOML parser for validation.
title: Init script TOML generation test harness

description: Pad all raw Ethernet frames to uniform 1024-byte blocks before transmission to prevent traffic analysis based on frame size. An attacker on the public mesh can distinguish 500-byte Reticulum announces from 2KB CAS Bloom filters by size alone. Padding removes this signal. Negligible bandwidth impact with mcast_rate=24000. Requires raw Ethernet transport.
title: Fixed frame padding for metadata obfuscation
